title: castalis tragus
intro: Castalis tragus, also known as Castor Bean or Castor Oil Plant, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and Africa. The plant's seeds contain ricin, a toxic compound, but the oil extracted from its seeds has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Castalis tragus have been used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. Castalis tragus oil can be prepared through cold-pressing or solvent extraction, and it is often used in herbal remedies, cosmetics, and pharmaceutical products.
